page: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8573C processor cores with hyper-threading operating at 4.2 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 260 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and includes 236 GB of local storage, with Premium Storage capability. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2165.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 31.43 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics show some limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 3984.38 and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression of 25.91 GB/sec fall into the lower performance tier. Memory read bandwidth is also limited, with uncached large block reads at 36.7 GB/sec and cached small block reads at 310.18 GB/sec. Conversely, large block memory write bandwidth of 25.67 GB/sec and Geekbench multi-core performance of 4845.0 maintain average tier results.

Given its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the D4ds_v6 offers balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads. The inclusion of 236 GB of local storage provides a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring fast temporary storage without the overhead of external disks. It is suitable for tasks such as static web serving, text processing, and light database operations, as reflected in its average Passmark database score of 2889.27 and static web serving throughput of 7.73 GB/sec. It can also handle small-scale LLM inference, processing 498.69 tokens per second for a 135M model, though it is less suited for heavy multi-threaded compilation or memory-intensive read operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv6 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. It features 236 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2165.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 31.43 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3984.38 and uncached large block read bandwidth of 36.7 GB/sec. This instance is suitable for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, text processing, and moderate database operations that benefit from local storage but do not require intensive multi-threaded scaling.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 236 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance with some multi-core tradeoffs.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 236 GB local storage, optimized for single-core workloads.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance featuring high single-core clock speeds, integrated local storage, and balanced memory allocation for standard workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory and 236 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2165.0
Moderate multi-core and memory read bandwidth performance under heavy parallel loads
Suitable for static web serving, text processing, and light database operations
